Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}}{{jalink|Asuka|Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{jalink|Achi no Omi}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{jalink|October 9|Q2923}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Asuka|Q752397}}, {{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, formerly ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It stands on the site of '''{{jalink|Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{jalink|Achi no Omi}} - ancestral deities of the {{jalink|Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{jalink|Achi no Omi}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}. In the late {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}, {{jalink|Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640}} was built as the {{jalink|ujidera|Q11547815}} of the {{jalink|Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{jalink|enshrined deities|Q11591100}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{jalink|Motoori Norinaga|Q384647}} visited in {{jalink|1772|Q7690}} ({{jalink|Meiwa|Q1146161}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}}'s {{jalink|Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine}} * {{jalink|Kasuga deity|Q11088805|Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|Konpira Daigongen Shrine}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665|Inari Daimyojin Shrine}} * {{jalink|Zao Gongen|Q10514059|Zao Daigongen Shrine}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{jalink|1970|Q2474}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Takaichi District|Q1117901}}{{jalink|Asuka|Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{jalink|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085}}{{jalink|Asuka Station|Q4811860}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{jalink|Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849}} * {{jalink|Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya}} * Ruins of {{jalink|Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640}} * {{jalink|Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447}} * {{jalink|Ani Shrine|Q11450252}} * {{jalink|Achi Shrine (Kurashiki)|Q11657446}} * {{jalink|Ashi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.asukabito.or.jp/spotDetail2.html Hinokuma-dera Ruins / Omiashi Shrine (Koto Asuka Preservation Foundation)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみあししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:東漢氏|社おみあししんしや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}'s {{ill||lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill||lt=Yasaka Shrine|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849}} * {{ill||lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447}} * {{ill|lt=Ani Shrine|Q11450252|WD=Q11450252}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Kurashiki)|Q11657446|WD=Q11657446}} * {{ill||lt=Ashi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} === External links === * [http://www.asukabito.or.jp/spotDetail2.html Hinokuma-dera Ruins / Omiashi Shrine (Koto Asuka Preservation Foundation)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみあししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:東漢氏|社おみあししんしや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447}} * {{ill|lt=Ani Shrine|Q11450252|WD=Q11450252}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Kurashiki)|Q11657446|WD=Q11657446}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ashi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} === External links === * [http://www.asukabito.or.jp/spotDetail2.html Hinokuma-dera Ruins / Omiashi Shrine (Koto Asuka Preservation Foundation)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみあししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:東漢氏|社おみあししんしや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447}} * {{ill|lt=Ani Shrine|Q11450252|WD=Q11450252}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Kurashiki)|Q11657446|WD=Q11657446}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === * [http://www.asukabito.or.jp/spotDetail2.html Hinokuma-dera Ruins / Omiashi Shrine (Koto Asuka Preservation Foundation)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみあししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:東漢氏|社おみあししんしや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447}} * {{ill|lt=Ani Shrine|Q11450252|WD=Q11450252}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Kurashiki)|Q11657446|WD=Q11657446}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links === * [http://www.asukabito.or.jp/spotDetail2.html Hinokuma-dera Ruins / Omiashi Shrine (Koto Asuka Preservation Foundation)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おみあししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Omiashi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:東漢氏|社おみあししんしや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Jinja.jpg|270px|Omiashi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849|1=Asuka-Fujiwara}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}} * Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === Jinja.jpg|270px|Omiashi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849|1=Asuka-Fujiwara}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Jinja.jpg|270px|Omiashi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849|1=Asuka-Fujiwara}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}} * It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === Jinja.jpg|270px|Omiashi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Association|authorlink=日本の神社研究会|title=100 Selected Shrines of Japan: Stages of Ancient History Worth Visiting Once|publisher=Takarajimasha|series=Takarajimasha Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849|1=Asuka-Fujiwara}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}} * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 23 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Seated wooden statues{{Refnest|group=注釈|The male deity is 1 shaku 2 sun; the female deity is 1 shaku 1 sun.}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (possibly shortly after the death of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper roofing |別名= |札所等= |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事= }} '''Omiashi Shrine''' (おみあしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the site of '''{{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}}'''. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities, husband and wife, of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} - ancestral deities of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. === History === This area is said to have been the residence site of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Achi no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who came to Japan in the reign of the 15th emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. In the late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}, {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} was built as the {{ill|lt=ujidera|Q11547815|WD=Q11547815|1=Clan temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Yamato-no-Aya clan|Q10870550|WD=Q10870550|1=Yamatonoaya clan}}. Later, west of the present site across the road, this shrine was founded as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the Yamato-no-Aya clan, with Achi no Omi and his wife as {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}{{Sfn|日本の神社研究会|2018|page=142}}, and as the guardian shrine of Hinokuma-dera. By the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Hinokuma-dera was called Doko-ji, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} this shrine was called Goryo Myojin. When {{ill|lt=Motoori Norinaga|Q384647|WD=Q384647|1=Motoori Norinaga}} visited in {{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}} ({{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|WD=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 9), only a temporary hut remained at Hinokuma-dera and old roof tiles were scattered in the precincts; Hinokuma-dera was later abolished. In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40), the main hall was relocated to the former site of Hinokuma-dera. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}'s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} stood in this area, though this is uncertain. === Precincts === * Main hall * Worship hall * Site of Hinokuma-dera lecture hall - foundation stones remain. * Eleven-tier stone pagoda ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - believed originally to have been a thirteen-tier pagoda made in the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The upper part is damaged, so it is now eleven tiers. It stands on the former pagoda platform of Hinokuma-dera. * Hinokuma-dera pagoda site - platform remains. The nearby central foundation stone is a replica. * Shrine office === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Daimyojin Shrine|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} * Fuji Daigongen * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|lt=Konpira Daigongen Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Chinju Daigongen Shrine * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Daimyojin Shrine|1=Inari Ōkami}} * {{ill|lt=Zao Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|lt=Zao Daigongen Shrine|1=Zaō Gongen}} Omiashi-jinja Hinokumaderaato 201911.jpg|Hinokuma-dera lecture hall site and Omiashi Shrine buildings Hinokuma-dera Temple Ruins.jpg|Eleven-tier stone pagoda === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Omiashi Shrine stone stupa, with associated offering implements (small glass jar, qingbai lidded container, four-eared jar with lid) - the eleven-tier stone pagoda. Designated on April 5, {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42); associated items additionally designated on June 17, {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45){{Cite web|和書|url= https://asukamura.jp/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_11.html |title=Cultural Properties |publisher=Asuka Village |accessdate=2019-09-13 }}. === Historic site === * Hinokuma-dera ruins === Festivals === * Renza Festival - May 15 * Autumn Festival - October 9 === Site information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Hinokuma 594 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Q901085|WD=Q901085|1=Yoshino Line}}{{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}}, about 15 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|author=Japan Shrine Research Shinsho|date=2018-5|isbn=978-4-8002-8431-0|ref=harv}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Asuka-Fujiwara palatial capital and related assets|Q4811849|WD=Q4811849|1=Asuka-Fujiwara}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinokuma Iorino-no-Miya|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ruins of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma-dera|Q11544640|WD=Q11544640|1=Hinokuma-dera}} * {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|WD=Q11657447|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}} *
